sourceMovie.title: Buried
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Buried (2010) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Bird Box
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Buried clicked for you, Bird Box (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: War of the Worlds
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Buried clicked for you, War of the Worlds (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Buried?
faq.answer: Try Bird Box, War of the Worlds, Twelve Monkeys—each shares DNA with Buried while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Buried?
faq.answer: Buried (2010) is best described as Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Buried worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
